name: Pint of View Lecture: Who Owns Bengaluru?—Participatory Citizenship & Urban Local Govt in Bengaluru
description:
Welcome to Pint of View, Bangalore’s first-ever community where classroom-style lectures meet drinks in pubs and bars.

About the Lecture

Why does Bengaluru feel increasingly difficult to live in, and who gets to decide what the city becomes? 

This lecture explores the idea of the city as a shared democratic project rather than a space that is simply administered from above. Through the history of local government in India, the recent Greater Bengaluru Governance Act, and the principles of participatory citizenship, architect and civic thinker Prem Chandavarkar examines how power is distributed, why urban governance matters, and what ordinary citizens can do to shape the future of their neighbourhoods and their city.

Whether you are frustrated by traffic, disappearing public spaces or planning failures, this lecture offers a new way to understand Bengaluru.

The lecture will tackle this through a framework of four questions: 

  • What is a city?

  • How has urban governance evolved in India?

  • What is the situation in Bengaluru?

  • What can you do?

About the Speaker

Prem Chandavarkar is the Managing Partner of CnT Architects, a legacy practice with a history dating back to being Bengaluru’s first architectural firm.

He is a former Executive Director of Srishti Manipal Institute of Art Design & Technology, is an academic advisor and guest faculty at Indian and international colleges of architecture, and was a founding trustee of Civic Bangalore, an activist NGO working for participatory governance in Bengaluru. He is also an internationally published writer on architecture and urbanism.

What to Expect:

• A 60-minute lecture

• 30–45 minutes of interactive Q&A

• One complimentary Geist beer + an appetiser/mocktail

Event Details:

📅 Wednesday, 17 June | 7 PM – 9 PM

📍 Fandom at Gilly’s Redefined, Koramangala

  • If you’re 21 or under 21, you will be allowed in the venue with an older guardian only.

  • Seating will be strictly on first-come first-served basis.

About Pint of View

Inspired by the Lectures on Tap communities in the U.S., Pint of View brings this format to India for the first time. It’s where big ideas meet bar tables, and where you walk in for a drink and walk out with a new way of seeing the world.

Book now & develop your own pint of view! 🍻
